Swan Song third-party notices
=============================

This file identifies grants and notices that can be proved from the pinned
source history. It does not declare a license for Regionally Famous's original
contributions and does not authorize distribution of the combined bitstream.
The machine-readable LICENSE-MANIFEST.json records the unresolved release
review separately.

WonderSwan FPGA core
--------------------

Copyright (c) 2021 Robert Peip.
MiSTer Framework copyright (C) 2021 Sorgelig.

MiSTer commit 8f7a4d670b4635eda0e518e7fd9a17ef8610db79 retains the
WonderSwan.sv program notice offering that program under the GNU General
Public License, version 2 or (at the recipient's option) any later version.
Swan Song descends through agg23/openfpga-wonderswan commit
073213a2e5992cff23b174d17763cb6354ee862b. That Pocket adaptation omitted the
program notice from wonderswan.sv; Swan Song restores the original copyright,
GPL-2.0-or-later, warranty, and source-delivery notice at the top of its current
Pocket-derived wrapper. The historical omission is not treated as a new grant.
The 18 files in the pinned WonderSwan program scope that differ from upstream
carry a prominent notice that Regionally Famous modified them for Swan Song on
2026-07-14. The source-bound license validator requires this audited
changed-file set to retain the exact dated notice text.

The GNU GPL version 2 text is in COPYING-GPL-2.0.txt.

MiSTer memory controllers
-------------------------

src/fpga/core/rtl/ddram.sv is copyright (c) 2019 Sorgelig.
src/fpga/core/rtl/sdram.sv is copyright (c) 2015-2019 Sorgelig.

Their retained headers offer each source file under the GNU General Public
License, version 3 or (at the recipient's option) any later version.
The modified sdram.sv also carries a prominent notice that Regionally Famous
modified it for Swan Song on 2026-07-14; the source-bound license validator
requires that notice to remain present.

The GNU GPL version 3 text is in COPYING-GPL-3.0.txt.

Analogue Pocket utilities
-------------------------

data_loader.sv, data_unloader.sv, sound_i2s.sv, and sync_fifo.sv descend from
agg23/analogue-pocket-utils commit
78482d1b363606336f4535aa0adc2e957bc20558. Their retained notices grant the
MIT License and identify Adam Gastineau as copyright holder.

The complete notice is in LICENSE-MIT-Adam-Gastineau.txt.

Chip32 assembler/image
----------------------

The packaged chip32.bin is assembled from Swan Song's checked-in
src/support/chip32.asm using the instruction encoding published by
open-fpga/bass-chip32 v1.0.0, commit
f3ad17dfd541e67b527298808ebef131e979ded4. That repository is offered under
the MIT License and identifies Peter Lemon as copyright holder.

The complete notice is in LICENSE-MIT-Peter-Lemon.txt.

Analogue Platform Framework
---------------------------

The source tree descends from open-fpga/core-template commit
da3a021b1eaf742604d86d8dc9b33a6666263e6a. APF source files retain Analogue's
Software License Agreement notice, including the incorporated EULA reference
and the clause stating that applicable MIT or GNU GPL terms prevail where
inconsistent.

The link resolves to Analogue's August 14, 2022 EULA. Its section 2 permits
developers to market, sell, or distribute Developer Applications while
separately restricting publication, copying, and modification of Analogue
Software. The complete retained APF notice is in NOTICE-Analogue-APF.txt.
Clarification of modified APF source redistribution and final combined terms
remains open.

Intel / Altera generated material
---------------------------------

The source tree includes Intel/Altera generated IP and reference-design files
whose embedded notices refer to separate applicable agreements. See
NOTICE-Intel-FPGA.txt. Its presence does not resolve that review.

Not included in the Pocket package
----------------------------------

Commercial game ROMs, Bandai BIOS images, WonderWitch firmware, save data, and
the repository's test ROM corpus are not included in this Pocket package.
The 19 legacy MiSTer test assets that previously occupied
testroms/spritepriority, testroms/timingtest, and testroms/windowtest were
removed from Swan Song. Project-authored deterministic REP MOVSB,
sprite-priority, and window-boundary probes now provide that regression
coverage without carrying those inherited bytes.

Corresponding source
--------------------

The project repository is:

  https://github.com/RegionallyFamous/swan-song

Release packaging records the exact source commit in the package provenance.
The release gate separately requires the corresponding source for that exact
commit to be made available with clear retrieval directions. This paragraph
is a pointer, not a written offer and not a conclusion that any source-delivery
obligation has been satisfied.
